participants.reasoning: The extracted cohort and eligibility criteria match the official trial description, including age, GAA deficiency/mutations, walking and FVC requirements, key exclusions, and the randomized 2:1 alglucosidase alfa/placebo design with the cardiovascular substudy’s exclusion of participants under 17 for adult norms.
participants.extracted: Adults and a small number of adolescents with late-onset Pompe disease (glycogen storage disease type II) were enrolled in a multicenter randomized controlled trial and then analyzed in this post-hoc cardiovascular substudy. Eligibility required age ≥8 years, deficient acid alpha-glucosidase activity, at least two GAA mutations, ability to walk 40 meters on two 6-minute walk tests, ability to perform pulmonary function testing, and upright forced vital capacity between 30% and 80% predicted. Exclusions included prior enzyme replacement therapy, current invasive or awake upright ventilatory support, recent investigational therapy, and other major conditions interfering with compliance. Ninety patients were randomized 2:1 to alglucosidase alfa or placebo, and after excluding three participants younger than 17 years because adult ECG/echocardiographic norms were used, 87 patients remained for the cardiovascular analysis (28 control, 59 ERT). The analyzed cohort had a median age of 44 years, was about half male, and was predominantly Caucasian.
participants.official:
90 participants with LOPD enroled and randomised. 81 completed the trial.
60 randomised to alglucosidase alfa group, 30 randomised to placebo group.
Alglucosidase alfa group: mean (SD) age at first infusion: 45.3 (12.4) years. Females: 26 (43%) and males: 34 (57%).
Placebo group: mean (SD) age at first infusion: 42.6 (11.6) years. Females: 19 (63%); males: 11 (37%).
Inclusion criteria*
Patient must provide signed, informed consent prior to performing any trial‐related procedures.

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention details, including alglucosidase alfa 20 mg/kg every other week for 78 weeks and a placebo control arm.
interventions.extracted: Patients were randomized 2:1 to receive bi-weekly infusions of enzyme replacement therapy with alglucosidase alfa (recombinant human acid alpha-glucosidase derived from Chinese hamster ovary cells) at a dose of 20 mg/kg for 78 weeks, or a volume-matched placebo infusion. Only the treatment arm received active enzyme replacement.
interventions.official:
Alglucosidase alfa group: IV infusions of alglucosidase alfa at 20 mg/kg of body weight every other week for 78 weeks.
Placebo group: IV infusions of placebo every other week for 78 weeks.

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes add respiratory measures like FEV and generic treatment-related reactions, but the official outcomes specify TEAEs, 6MWT, % predicted FVC, pharmacokinetics, and secondary QMT/SF-36 measures, so the sets do not match.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure the following outcomes: 6-minute Walk Test, assessed as walking distance in meters at baseline and during follow-up, with the primary time point at 78 weeks; Forced Vital Capacity (FVC), assessed as percent-predicted upright FVC at baseline and during follow-up, with the primary time point at 78 weeks; respiratory function outcomes including changes in pulmonary function testing parameters such as FVC and related respiratory measures over the study period to 78 weeks; forced expiratory volume (FEV), assessed by pulmonary function testing during follow-up; and infusion-site or treatment-related reactions, assessed as adverse events occurring at any time during the 78-week treatment period.
outcomes.official:
Primary outcome measures
Summary of participants reporting TEAEs (time frame: weeks 0 ‐ 78)
Mean distance walked as measured by 6MWT at weeks 0 and 78, and mean change from baseline (time frame: weeks 0, 78)
% predicted FVC (time frame: weeks 0, 78)
Alglucosidase alfa pharmacokinetic parameters: AUC (time frame: weeks 0, 12, and 52)
Alglucosidase alfa pharmacokinetic parameters: mean maximum plasma concentration (Cmax) (time frame: weeks 0, 12, 52)
